MarkdownEditor.externalChangeDescription: The file has been modified externally. Do you want to apply the changes?
MarkdownEditor.clearConfirm: Delete all content? This action cannot be undone.
MarkdownEditor.importConfirm: Current content will be replaced. Import file?
MarkdownEditor.noPermission: You do not have permission to view this page.
MarkdownEditor.altTextGuidance: Briefly describe the image content for screen readers
MarkdownEditor.draggableHeading: Draggable heading, use Alt+Arrow to reorder
MarkdownEditor.deleteFrontmatterConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the frontmatter?
MarkdownEditor.resetSettingsConfirm: Reset all settings to default values?
MarkdownEditor.plantumlExternalWarning: PlantUML diagrams are rendered by sending code to an external server (plantuml.com). Your diagram code will be transmitted over the internet.
MarkdownEditor.versionDescription: A markdown editor with real-time preview, diagram support (Mermaid, PlantUML), and rich editing features.
MarkdownEditor.versionTech: Built with Next.js, TipTap, Mermaid, PlantUML, Material UI
MarkdownEditor.versionCopyright: Copyright © Kiyotaka Ueda featuring Claude Code
MarkdownEditor.helpBasicOpsDesc: The editor supports two modes and various file operations.
MarkdownEditor.helpBasicOpsWysiwyg: Edit mode: Edit with rich-text formatting and real-time preview.
MarkdownEditor.helpBasicOpsSource: Source mode: Edit raw Markdown directly. Switch via the View menu.
MarkdownEditor.helpBasicOpsFile: File operations: Copy, Download (.md), Import (.md), and Clear from the toolbar.
MarkdownEditor.helpTextDecorationDesc: Apply formatting to selected text using the toolbar or bubble menu.
MarkdownEditor.helpTextDecorationBubble: Select text to show the bubble menu: Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Highlight, Inline Code, Link.
MarkdownEditor.helpTextDecorationToolbar: You can also use the toolbar buttons or keyboard shortcuts for text formatting.
MarkdownEditor.helpBlocksDesc: Insert block-level elements via the toolbar or keyboard shortcuts.
MarkdownEditor.helpBlocksHeading: Headings: H1 through H5 are available.
MarkdownEditor.helpBlocksList: Lists: Bullet list, Ordered list, and Task list (with checkboxes).
MarkdownEditor.helpBlocksQuote: Blockquote: Indent text as a quotation.
MarkdownEditor.helpBlocksCode: Code Block: Insert a fenced code block with optional language syntax highlighting.
MarkdownEditor.helpBlocksHr: Horizontal Rule: Insert a divider line.
MarkdownEditor.helpMarginMenuDesc: Click the label (H1, H2, P, UL, OL, Task, Quote) shown at the left margin of each block to open a menu for changing the block type (heading level, paragraph, list, blockquote).
MarkdownEditor.helpTableDesc: Insert and edit tables using the toolbar and in-table controls.
MarkdownEditor.helpTableInsert: Insert a table from the toolbar button.
MarkdownEditor.helpTableEdit: Add/remove columns and rows, set cell alignment (left/center/right) via the floating table toolbar.
MarkdownEditor.helpTableMove: Move rows up/down and columns left/right using the move buttons.
MarkdownEditor.helpTableNav: Navigate cells with Tab (next cell) and Shift+Tab (previous cell).
MarkdownEditor.helpTableFullscreen: Expand the table to fullscreen mode for easier editing of large tables.
MarkdownEditor.helpMermaidDesc: Create diagrams using Mermaid syntax in a code block.
MarkdownEditor.helpMermaidTypes: Supported diagram types: Flowchart, Sequence, Class, ER, State Machine, Gantt, Mindmap, Git Graph, and more.
MarkdownEditor.helpMermaidSamples: Insert diagram samples from the toolbar to get started quickly.
MarkdownEditor.helpMermaidTools: Use the floating toolbar for node/edge/participant insertion helpers.
MarkdownEditor.helpMermaidZoom: Zoom in/out and pan the rendered diagram. Use fullscreen mode for larger diagrams.
MarkdownEditor.helpPlantUmlDesc: Create diagrams using PlantUML syntax in a code block.
MarkdownEditor.helpPlantUmlTypes: Supported diagram types: Sequence, Class, Use Case, Activity, State, Component, Deployment, ER, and more.
MarkdownEditor.helpPlantUmlExternal: PlantUML diagrams are rendered via an external server (plantuml.com). You will be asked for consent before sending diagram code.
MarkdownEditor.helpPlantUmlSamples: Insert predefined samples from the floating toolbar to get started.
MarkdownEditor.helpPlantUmlTools: Use the floating toolbar for arrows, participants, classes, and other element insertion helpers.
MarkdownEditor.helpSearchDesc: Find and replace text within the editor.
MarkdownEditor.helpSearchOpen: Open the search bar from the toolbar or with the keyboard shortcut.
MarkdownEditor.helpSearchOptions: Options: Match Case, Match Whole Word, Use Regular Expression.
MarkdownEditor.helpSearchFullscreen: Search also works within fullscreen code editor and table views.
MarkdownEditor.helpOutlineDesc: View the document structure and navigate between sections.
MarkdownEditor.helpOutlineJump: Click a heading in the outline to jump to that position in the editor.
MarkdownEditor.helpOutlineFold: Fold/unfold sections to collapse or expand content under headings.
MarkdownEditor.helpOutlineMove: Reorder sections by drag-and-drop or up/down move buttons.
MarkdownEditor.helpOutlineResize: Drag the panel border to resize the outline panel width.
MarkdownEditor.helpSettingsDesc: Customize the editor appearance and behavior.
MarkdownEditor.helpSettingsItems: Adjustable settings: Line Height, Font Size, Table Width, Editor Min Width, Editor Background Color.
MarkdownEditor.helpShortcutsDesc: Use keyboard shortcuts for faster editing. The following shortcuts are available:
MarkdownEditor.helpImageDndDesc: Insert images and import Markdown files by various methods.
MarkdownEditor.helpImageDndUrl: Insert an image by specifying a URL via the toolbar button.
MarkdownEditor.helpImageDndDrop: Drag and drop image files directly into the editor.
MarkdownEditor.helpImageDndPaste: Paste images from the clipboard (Ctrl+V / Cmd+V).
MarkdownEditor.helpImageDndImport: Drag and drop a .md file into the editor to import its content.
MarkdownEditor.fileApiNotSupported: This browser does not support direct file access. Use Upload/Download instead.
MarkdownEditor.saveFileNoHandle: Open a file first to use Save
MarkdownEditor.embedPreviewEmpty: Enter a URL to see a preview.
MarkdownEditor.spreadsheetDiscardMessage: You have unapplied changes. Are you sure you want to close without applying?
MarkdownEditor.encodingChangeConfirm: Change encoding to {encoding}? Some characters may not display correctly.

Landing.heroDescription: A free, open-source markdown editor that works entirely in your browser. No sign-up, no server — your words stay yours.
Landing.noSignupRequired: No sign-up required — start writing instantly
Landing.featureWysiwygDesc: Write with rich formatting that instantly renders as you type. Bold, italic, links, images — all at your fingertips.
Landing.featureSourceDesc: Switch to raw Markdown editing with syntax highlighting. Full control over your document structure.
Landing.featureDiffDesc: Open two documents side by side, compare differences, and merge changes with a single click.
Landing.featureDiagramDesc: Create flowcharts, sequence diagrams, ER diagrams, and more with Mermaid and PlantUML support.
Landing.featurePdfDesc: Export your documents as beautifully formatted PDFs, ready for sharing or printing.
Landing.featurePwaDesc: Install as a progressive web app. Edit your documents anytime, anywhere — no internet required.
Landing.featureCommentDesc: Browse your document in read-only review mode. Add comments and toggle checkboxes without accidentally editing content.
Landing.featureOutlineDesc: Navigate your document structure at a glance. Jump to any heading instantly with the sidebar outline panel.
Landing.featureSlashDesc: Type / to quickly insert tables, code blocks, diagrams, and more — no need to remember toolbar locations.
Landing.featureTableDesc: Create and edit tables visually. Add, delete, and move rows and columns, change alignment — all without touching Markdown syntax.
Landing.featureMathDesc: Write LaTeX math formulas and see them rendered in real time with KaTeX. From simple equations to complex notation.
Landing.featureGithubDesc: Browse GitHub repositories directly from the editor. Open files, view Git history, and track changes without leaving the app.
Landing.experimentalNotice: This site is an experimental project where all design, implementation, and testing are performed by Claude Code, an AI coding assistant — humans provide only instructions. Anytime Markdown itself is used to review specifications and verify test plans during development. Source code is available on <github>GitHub</github>.
Landing.docsDeleteFolderConfirm: Delete this folder and all {count} file(s) inside?
Landing.docsUploadFolderOverwrite: Folder "{folder}" already exists ({count} file(s)). Delete existing files and overwrite?
Landing.docsEmptyHint: Add documents from the admin page

Common.savingWillCommit: Saving will commit to your GitHub repository
Common.historyMayBeStale: * History may not reflect the latest commits

VsCode.metaTitle: Visual Studio Code™ Extension - Anytime Markdown
VsCode.metaDescription: Rich Markdown editing in Visual Studio Code. WYSIWYG, diff highlighting, image annotation, and Git integration.
VsCode.heroDescription:
AI agents are a caravan crossing the harsh terrain of development.
WYSIWYG Markdown editing with diff review and real-time TypeScript architecture visualization — two VS Code extensions that serve as your compass in the age of AI.
VsCode.whyCamelTitle: Why a Camel? — Companionship, not Control
VsCode.whyCamelBody:
The horse gallops fast on paved roads but falters against the unknown or heavy context. The camel (AI agents) carries the entire codebase and thinks through sandstorms (errors) where faster tools falter — a long-distance runner. In the caravan, the camel is no beast of burden but a comrade who reads the leader's intent.

The prevailing approach tries to control AI with upfront constraints — whitelists, guardrails, sandboxes, tool restrictions. But the more you constrain it, the more its autonomy and judgment are lost. Anytime imposes no constraints. We make actions visible (Trail) and artifacts reviewable (Markdown), building trust through verification both before and after — a philosophy embodied in two extensions.
VsCode.editorCaption: No sign-up — start instantly in browser
VsCode.md1Title: Review AI's footprints. Lock what's done.
VsCode.md1Body: AI-edited sections are color-highlighted for instant diff comparison. Lock finalized sections to prevent AI from re-editing them.
VsCode.md2Body: Switch between WYSIWYG, Source, and Review modes with a single click. Review mode is read-only — perfect for focused review of AI output.
VsCode.md3Title: Preview docs and diagrams in VS Code
VsCode.md3Body: Instantly preview AI-generated Markdown in WYSIWYG. Mermaid, PlantUML, and math (KaTeX) render directly in the editor — no context switching needed.
VsCode.trail1Body: Analyze TypeScript projects to auto-generate C4 architecture diagrams and DSM (Dependency Structure Matrix). Drill down from system overview to individual files across four levels (L1–L4), with circular dependencies highlighted in red at a glance.
VsCode.trail2Body: Track error counts, retry rates, build/test failure rates, and compact-loop occurrences per session. Quantify AI agent work quality and quickly detect repeated failures or inefficient editing patterns. Undertested modules are also overlaid on the C4 diagram for at-a-glance review.
VsCode.trail3Title: Record Every AI Action — behavior visualization
VsCode.trail3Body: Visualize user input, AI responses, and tool executions turn by turn as a hierarchical tree, with tool call parameters and results in expandable nodes. A conversation tree synced with the turn timeline lets you trace exactly what the AI agent decided, when, and why.
VsCode.trail4Title: Visual AI Briefing — share visual context
VsCode.trail4Body: Paste screenshots and annotations into Agent Note, then run `/anytime-note [page] [task]` to issue work instructions that reference the note's images, text, and memos in a single command. Convey UI glitches or diagrams that text alone can't capture.
VsCode.trail5Body: Monitor total token consumption, estimated cost, and cache hit rate over time, with output ratio and context-growth signals at a glance. Productivity metrics per commit, per hour, and per line let you quantify AI agent ROI.
VsCode.trail6Body: Automatically measure lead time, deployment frequency, change failure rate, and release success rate. Quantify Four Keys-based delivery performance and accelerate continuous improvement with the release bar chart and DORA scorecards.
VsCode.trail7Title: Track Sub-agent Delegation — delegation structure visualization
VsCode.trail7Body: Visualize Task-tool sub-agent invocations on dedicated tracks along the timeline. See parallel execution, duration, and outcomes of delegated tasks at a glance — and verify exactly which decisions were entrusted to which agent.
VsCode.trail8Body: Overlay test coverage, complexity, defect risk, and hotspot heatmaps onto the C4 diagram. Locate quality weaknesses in their structural context and instantly judge refactoring priorities.
VsCode.trail9Title: Track AI Activity in Structure — behavior visualization
VsCode.trail9Body: Track in real time which C4 modules Claude Code agents are editing right now. Multi-agent parallel work is also visualized via multi-agent lanes, letting you grasp work concentration and collisions within the structure.
VsCode.trail10Title: Track Inter-release Structural Change — drift visualization
VsCode.trail10Body: Select releases to compare coverage diffs and dependency changes, detecting architectural decay structurally. Identify drifting areas and repair gaps between design and implementation early.
VsCode.trail11Body: Overlay Louvain-detected implementation communities onto the C4 diagram to surface gaps between designed boundaries and observed cohesion. Propose refactoring candidates and boundary redesigns grounded in structure.
VsCode.trail12Body: Run `/anytime-note 要約` to extract changes and pending tasks from `git log`, `git diff`, and the conversation, then write a new note page automatically. Mark clean checkpoints even in long sessions.
VsCode.trail13Title: Hand Off Across Sessions — context continuity
VsCode.trail13Body: Run `/anytime-note 引継ぎ` to load the latest summary page from a previous session and resume from pending tasks. Even after `/clear`, the AI grasps the recent history and the next steps to take.
VsCode.trail14Title: Replay AI Behavior on the Time Axis — time scrubber visualization
VsCode.trail14Body: Rewind graphs, commits, and edits to any point during a session. Replay the AI agent's decision sequence and reproduce the context just before an error or the progression of a refactor over time.
VsCode.trail15Title: Discover Temporally Coupled Code — hidden dependency visualization
VsCode.trail15Body: Extract file pairs frequently co-edited within a time window and overlay them on the C4 diagram as ghost edges. Surface hidden logical coupling that doesn't appear in the designed dependency graph and inform refactoring decisions.
VsCode.trail16Title: Prevent Concurrent Agent Collisions — air-space separation
VsCode.trail16Body: Agents submit a flight plan (work scope) up front, and the control tower detects scope breaches or parallel edits on the same file. Run multiple AI agents simultaneously without destructive overwrites or contradictory commits.
VsCode.trail17Title: Minimum Risk Maneuver on Anomaly — emergency stop protocol
VsCode.trail17Body: When unexpected errors, cost overruns, or destructive operations are detected, the aircraft autonomously triggers an MRM (Minimum Risk Maneuver) to move to a safe state. The control tower contains the blast radius by rerouting other aircraft.
VsCode.trail18Title: Multi-layer Defense for Persistent Data — preventing data destruction incidents
VsCode.trail18Body: A five-layer defense — type signatures, implementation conventions, test conventions, pre-commit hooks, and runtime monitoring — blocks unintended writes to user storage at every stage. Structurally prevent AI-induced data destruction incidents.
VsCode.trail19Body: Measure AI autonomy across four levels — L1 (manual approval), L2 (conditional autonomy), L3 (autonomy within ODD), L4 (full autonomy). Promote levels through gates as project maturity grows, raising autonomy safely.
VsCode.trail20Title: Autonomous Approval Within ODD — operational domain policy
VsCode.trail20Body: Within the Operational Design Domain (a predefined work scope), the AI proceeds with autonomous approval; the control tower is consulted only on scope breaches. Speed safe work through and focus human review on changes that need attention.
VsCode.trail21Title: Redundant Verification by Multiple Agents — second opinion visualization
VsCode.trail21Body: Delegate critical decisions to multiple AI agents in parallel and visualize consensus or divergence. Cancel out one aircraft's bias or hallucination with another's perspective and structurally raise decision reliability.
VsCode.trail22Title: Predict Defects from Past Patterns — defect prediction visualization
VsCode.trail22Body: Score likely future defect locations from past defect patterns, complexity, and change frequency. Overlay them on the C4 diagram as a risk map and prioritize testing and review based on structural signals.
VsCode.trail23Title: Discover Unused Code — Dead Code detection
VsCode.trail23Body: Extract code with no access or edits over a time window from time-axis data and mark Dead Code candidates. Surface implementations that can be safely removed, grounded in cross-release behavioral data.
VsCode.trail24Title: Manage Knowledge Generations — graph versioning visualization
VsCode.trail24Body: Store generations of the C4 model and code graph per release, allowing the structure at any point in time to be restored. Trace the evolution of design intent and reproduce when, why, and by whom the structure was changed.
VsCode.trail25Title: Surface Where Knowledge Lives — knowledge distribution visualization
VsCode.trail25Body: Infer from edit history which agents are familiar with which modules and topics, surfacing imbalances and blank spots in knowledge distribution. Useful for choosing review targets and detecting bus-factor risk areas early.
VsCode.trail26Title: Self-update from Experience — autonomous learning loop
VsCode.trail26Body: Visualize the loop in which the AI updates its policy autonomously from success and failure experiences. The Memory Shrink Guard prevents inadvertent shrinkage of the learning base, accumulating knowledge assets over the long run.
VsCode.feature1Body: Visually edit Markdown with real-time preview
VsCode.feature2Body: Edit raw Markdown directly. Switch instantly between WYSIWYG and source
VsCode.feature3Body: Type "/" to quickly insert headings, tables, code blocks, and diagrams
VsCode.feature4Body: Visualize structural changes with section-level diff, not just line-level
VsCode.feature5Body: Add annotations directly on images with rectangles, circles, lines, and text
VsCode.feature6Body: Capture screenshots and record GIFs within the editor
VsCode.feature7Body: Changes, commit graph, and timeline integrated in the sidebar
VsCode.feature8Body: Real-time warnings for broken links and missing anchors
VsCode.feature9Body: Inline comments and review mode for efficient team collaboration
VsCode.feature10Body: Overview document structure from headings and jump with one click
VsCode.feature11Body: Insert design docs, API specs, and Markdown references with one command
VsCode.feature12Body: UI supports Japanese and English switching

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.section1Body: Anytime Markdown ("the App") is a browser-based markdown editor. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle information when you use the App.
Privacy.section2Body: The App does not collect personal information. The App runs locally on your device. Your documents and settings are stored only on your device and are not transmitted to our servers.
Privacy.section3Body: All data created within the App (documents, settings, preferences) is stored locally on your device using browser storage. We do not have access to this data. The App uses localStorage for language and theme preferences.
Privacy.section4Intro: The App may connect to the following external services for specific features:
Privacy.section4Plantuml: PlantUML Server — When rendering PlantUML diagrams, after obtaining user consent, diagram code is sent to an external PlantUML rendering server. No personal information is included in these requests.
Privacy.section4Mermaid: Mermaid Diagrams — Mermaid diagram rendering is performed entirely within the browser. No data is sent to external servers.
Privacy.section5Body1: The App may use Google Analytics, as configured by the operator, to collect anonymous usage data such as page views and session duration. This data is used solely to improve the App and is not used for advertising or sold to third parties. No personally identifiable information is collected through analytics.
Privacy.section5Body2: The App does not use tracking pixels or advertising SDKs.
Privacy.section6Body: The App does not knowingly collect information from children under 13. The App is a general-purpose text editor suitable for all ages.
Privacy.section7Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be reflected on this page with an updated revision date.
Privacy.section8Body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us through our <link>GitHub Issues</link>.
